fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 17-year-old female brought by EMS after ingesting 30 acetaminophen tablets with clear intent to die.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 17 y/o female who presents via ambulance following intentional ingestion of approximately 30 acetaminophen 500mg tablets approximately 4 hours prior to arrival. Per father and patient report, she left a detailed note explaining that she could no longer handle the pressure of being the 'perfect daughter' and that her family would be disappointed to learn she was not accepted to any of her top choice universities. The note explicitly stated her intent to end her life. Father discovered her unresponsive in her bedroom and called 911 immediately. Patient was medically stabilized and received activated charcoal and N-acetylcysteine protocol. She is now medically stable for psychiatric evaluation. During interview, patient remains tearful and expresses continued hopelessness about her future, stating she has 'ruined everything' and has 'no purpose anymore.' She describes months of increasing anxiety, perfectionism, and difficulty sleeping, compounded by recent college rejections. She reports active ongoing suicidal ideation with both plan and intent to die, stating she wishes the attempt had been successful. She has been on fluoxetine and buspirone for anxiety but feels these have not helped. Denies any identifiable protective factors at this time. Prior psychiatric hospitalization one year ago for similar ideation. (Psychiatrist Dr. Steven Park, MD)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Disheveled, hospital gown, IV in place
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Active SI with ongoing plan and intent, recent high-lethality attempt

fields.CURES_Report: Active prescriptions for fluoxetine and buspirone on file

Laboratory_Data.Acetaminophen_Level: Elevated, trending down on NAC protocol

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Major depressive disorder, severe, with suicide attempt; generalized anxiety disorder.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with both parents; high academic achiever under significant parental and self-imposed pressure; previous psychiatric hospitalization; limited peer support system; currently on psychotropic medications.
Assessment_and_Plan: High acute risk: recent high-lethality suicide attempt with ongoing SI, plan, and intent.
Minimal to no protective factors identified; poor insight and judgment.
Medical stabilization ongoing with NAC protocol and LFT monitoring.
5150 criteria met; inpatient psychiatric admission required immediately upon medical clearance.
Medication reassessment needed; consider higher level of antidepressant therapy or augmentation.
1:1 observation maintained; restrict access to all potential means of self-harm.
fields.Author: Dr. Steven Park, MD (Psychiatrist)
